title: SPECKIT_*_STATE_DIR does not actually isolate the native CLI lock; comment overstates the guarantee

findingDetails.evidence: fanout-run.cjs:416-419 sets SPECKIT_CODEX_STATE_DIR=<unique stateDir>, but it is never mapped to CODEX_HOME/OPENCODE_HOME/CLAUDE_CODE_HOME and is dropped by buildExecutorDispatchEnv's per-kind prefix allowlist (executor-audit.ts:86-90,551-558), so the native CLI never sees it and writes its real lock to the shared default home. getDefaultStatePaths (executor-audit.ts:184-188) always also scans $HOME/.<home>; detectFromLockfile only matches speckit-guessed lock names (executor-audit.ts:196-199) that nothing in-repo writes, so the layer is inert. The comment 'Isolate per-kind state dirs so same-kind replicas do not collide on lockfiles' is not delivered by this var.
findingDetails.why: A load-bearing safety comment claims same-kind replicas are isolated against lockfile collision via this env var, but the only real isolation comes from the unique lineage.label artifact dir. If a future change made detectFromLockfile match a real CLI lock name in the shared home, concurrent same-kind fan-out would silently self-block (false-positive recursion-guard, recorded as dispatch_failure) — a latent under-fanout risk the comment hides.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either map SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR to the CLI's real home env (so the native lock relocates per replica) — weighing the auth-dir tradeoff in executor-audit.ts:78-84 — or rewrite the comment to state the layer is detection-only and that real-lock isolation relies on the CLI's own shared home, removing the false 'do not collide on lockfiles' guarantee.

title: Test named for same-kind-replica lock isolation does not exercise the replica (count>1) path

findingDetails.evidence: The test uses two distinct base labels (alpha, beta) with count:1, not a single label with count>1 that triggers expandLineages's `${label}-${replica}` path (executor-config.ts:333-335). Its assertions (fanout-run.vitest.ts:226-230) only check that two filesystem dirs exist and differ; despite the comment at :186, it never asserts the stub codex's echoed STATE_DIR/LINEAGE_ID values were distinct.
findingDetails.why: The regression guard's name claims it protects same-kind-replica lockfile isolation, but it neither covers the count-expansion code path (the actual same-kind-replica scenario) nor verifies env propagation — so a regression in expandLineages label suffixing or in the extraEnv injection could pass undetected.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a case with a single executor {label:'rep', kind:'cli-codex', count:2} asserting lineages/rep-1 and lineages/rep-2 each receive a distinct SPECKIT_CODEX_STATE_DIR (assert on the stub's captured stdout, not just dir existence).
